title: Shorshe Maach (Bengali Mustard Fish)

ingredients.name: rohu, katla or bhetki fish steaks

subs.name: firm tofu slabs (200 g) for a vegetarian adaptation
subs.notes: pat dry and fry tofu 3 minutes per side

ingredients.name: white mustard seeds (shorshe)
visual_checks: soak in water 15 minutes before grinding
ingredients.notes: soak in water 15 minutes before grinding

subs.name: 2 tbsp ready-made yellow mustard paste from a jar
subs.notes: reduce the water to 80 ml as the paste is already fluid

subs.name: refined oil
subs.notes: the dish will taste noticeably milder; add an extra tbsp of mustard paste to compensate

steps.text: Soak the mustard seeds in 4 tablespoons of water for 15 minutes so they grind smoothly. Rub the fish with half the turmeric and a pinch of salt and set aside.

Drain and grind the soaked mustard seeds with 2 green chillies, remaining turmeric and a pinch of salt using a small blender or mortar. Add just enough water to bring it to a thick, smooth paste — about 50 ml.
Heat mustard oil in a kadai over high flame until it lightens in colour. Carefully shallow-fry the fish steaks until pale golden on both sides, about 2 minutes per side. Remove and rest on a plate.
Lower the flame to medium. Pour the mustard paste into the same kadai and stir for 1 minute to cook out the raw edge. Add the red chilli powder if using, and the remaining 2 slit green chillies.
Pour in 150 ml water, season with salt and bring to a gentle simmer. Carefully place the fried fish into the gravy in a single layer.
Cover and cook on low for 8 minutes. The mustard paste will thicken the gravy and the fish will absorb the pungent flavour. Do not stir — slide the kadai gently if needed.
Uncover, drizzle 1 teaspoon of raw mustard oil over the top as a finishing note, and serve hot directly over steamed rice. Shorshe maach waits for no one.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: fish → firm tofu slabs (200 g) for a vegetarian adaptation (pat dry and fry tofu 3 minutes per side); mustard-seeds → 2 tbsp ready-made yellow mustard paste from a jar (reduce the water to 80 ml as the paste is already fluid); mustard-oil → refined oil (the dish will taste noticeably milder; add an extra tbsp of mustard paste to compensate)
